claim: Observability for AI agents requires open telemetry traces (audit records of agent actions) plus sessions (state over time), not just code review; telemetry captures what agents actually did.
actionable: Instrument your agent with OpenTelemetry from the start; traces will capture the actual execution path and tool order that code review cannot detect.

claim: Agent evals come in five flavors: LLM-as-judge, human feedback, golden datasets, deterministic checks, and business metrics; each has different costs and signal quality.
actionable: Choose eval types based on cost and domain: use deterministic checks for schema validation, golden datasets for quality calibration, business metrics for ROI.

claim: Evals should be scoped to the right level: span evals (single LLM call), multi-span evals (data across components), trajectory evals (tool call order), and session evals (conversation state).
actionable: Don't eval everything; start with trajectory evals (did tools run in the right order?) and session evals (was the user satisfied?); add lower scopes as needed.

claim: Trajectory evals reveal ordering bugs: if an agent calls tool B before A and B depends on A, telemetry shows this; traditional code audits miss non-deterministic execution paths.
actionable: Use trajectory evals to catch tool ordering bugs; add validation rules that check dependencies (e.g., 'A must run before B') and alert on violations.

claim: Improvements in one agent component can cause regressions in others; observability + evals must track the full system to avoid hidden failures.
actionable: When changing agent components, re-run full-system evals across multiple scopes; a fix in one tool doesn't guarantee other paths still work.

claim: Arize Phoenix is open-source, single-container, and requires no Kubernetes; enterprise Arize AX adds automated eval generation via Alex (AI system) that scans traces and suggests evals.
actionable: Start with open-source Phoenix for local agent observability; graduate to Arize AX if you need automated eval discovery and trace analysis.

claim: The future of agent observability is automation: AI systems should generate evals, detect anomalies, and recommend fixes without manual dashboard work.
actionable: Plan for future agent observability systems that generate evals and catch issues automatically; avoid building permanent manual workflows.
